figures.caption: FIG. 4: Frequency gap ∆ω at k = 0.75nm−1 (a) and intensity ratio between both sublattices along the two different branches (b) of the magnon dispersion relation. The model (dashed lines) is described in Section II C and Ref.10, while data points are obtained with mumax+. The angular coordinate in (a) corresponds to the relative angle φ between the exchange matrix eigenbasis and the simulation grid. Simulation details are described in Section II C, but the total simulation time T is increased to 200ps to improve the frequency resolution.

FIG. 3: The dispersion relation of magnons in a 1D altermagnet as described in Section II C. The angle φ between the exchange matrix eigenbasis and the simulation grid is set to 0◦ in the top panel and 45◦ in the bottom panel. The wave vector k lies along the length axis of the magnet. The dashed lines correspond to the model Eq. (6), while the intensity plot data is obtained with mumax+.

FIG. 1: Domain wall profiles of the z-component of the sublattice magnetizations of an altermagnet with uncoupled sublattices. The double arrows and dashed lines indicate twice the domain wall widths ∆1 and ∆2 for sublattice 1 and 2 respectively, obtained from fitting the Walker solution Eq. (4). The exchange matrix eigenvalues are chosen as A1 = 50pJ/m = 10A2 to clearly visualize the difference in the domain wall widths. Further details are explained in Section II B.

FIG. 2: Profiles of the Néel vector (top) and the net magnetization (bottom) of an altermagnet as modeled by Gomonay et al. 10 (dashed line) and simulated using mumax+ (points) for a Bloch domain wall (ϕ =−π/2) with width ∆. More details are explained in Section II B.
